name: Elvin Jones
abstract: Elvin Ray Jones (September 9, 1927 – May 18, 2004) was an American jazz drummer of the post-bop era. Most famously a member of John Coltrane's quartet, with whom he recorded from late 1960 to late 1965, Jones appeared on such albums as My Favorite Things, A Love Supreme, Ascension, and Live at Birdland.

quotes.text: Well I don’t think so. If I can continue what I’m doing that’ll satisfy me because I’m not that ambitious. I don’t think, “Hey it’s time for me to play at Carnegie Hall or something.” That’s just silly. Playing in Central Park is just as important, and its probably more rewarding because then the birds can hear you. (Laughs).
quotes.caption: Elvin Jones dismisses playing Carnegie Hall as silly
quotes.doc_title: Elvin Jones — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Owens
about_quotes.evidence: I went back to Japan a little later in that year with a special drum tour that had Art Blakey, Elvin Jones, Tony Williams, McCoy Tyner, Ben Tucker played bass, Wayne Shorter and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Owens interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Foster
about_quotes.evidence: shortly after leaving Basie I formed a big band with him the drummer... He just asked me to play with him... At least four recordings with Elvin.
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Foster — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Owens
about_quotes.evidence: It was Tony Williams, Elvin Jones, Art Blakey, McCoy Tyner, Ben Tucker on bass, Wayne Shorter and myself. We played for about a week...
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Owens — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jason Marsalis
about_quotes.evidence: I eventually did meet him. I was very honored. ... I can’t wait to hear you tonight. ... I eventually did meet him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jason Marsalis — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Neal Caine
about_quotes.evidence: I was lucky enough to play with Elvin Jones and his band for three or four years
about_quotes.doc_title: Neal Caine interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Hartford, New York, September 26, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: John Abercrombie
about_quotes.evidence: I haven’t played with Elvin Jones. And I just think that’s got to be great.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Abercrombie interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, April 19, 2001
